title: AI Toolbox Hook Protocol
description: Schema for .agent/contracts/hook-protocol.json — defines per-client what hooks may receive, return, and modify. The 'guarantees' block makes implicit assumptions explicit so adapters can be reasoned about formally.

clients.description: Map of client identifier to its hook protocol definition. Identifiers must match keys in .ai-toolbox/config.json clients{}.

guarantees.description: What the hook system can and cannot do for this client. False here is a hard system limit, not a current implementation gap.

can_block.description: Hook can prevent the tool call from running by returning non-zero or a structured deny.

can_modify_input.description: Hook can rewrite the tool input before execution.

can_modify_output.description: Hook can rewrite the tool output after execution.

can_inject_context.description: Hook can add additional context (system prompts, files, prefixes) into the model's window.

events.description: Hook event names supported by this client. Each event entry documents stdin format and exit-code semantics.

settings_path.description: Where the client expects hook configuration to live (e.g. .qwen/settings.json, .codex/hooks.json).

stdin_format.description: Description of the JSON shape (or 'none') the client passes on stdin.

stdout_format.description: Expected stdout shape. Use 'plain text' or 'ignored' for clients that do not consume stdout.

exit_codes.description: Map of exit code (as string) to its semantic meaning for this event.

env_vars.description: Environment variables the client sets when invoking the hook. Useful for non-stdin metadata.

implementation.description: Path to the script implementing this event in the toolbox.
